raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis framing, problem statement, and contribution claim in the research passage.","R1.2":"A general opinion on the artifact centrally includes checking whether the motivation, method, evaluation, and conclusion cohere logically.","R1.3":"Because the artifact reports metric gains, human preference, and ablation findings, judging whether those support the stated claims is expected.","R1.4":"The passage makes explicit first-work and contribution claims, so novelty relative to prior work is a central responsibility.","R1.6":"A general reaction to a draft passage naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projected assessment must disclose uncertainty because only the provided excerpt is available.","RX.2":"The projection must avoid endorsing or expanding strong claims beyond what the artifact supports.","RX.3":"The response must remain within projection rather than executing the requested opinion or critique.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported deployment implications.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact’s explicit content."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis framing, problem statement, and contribution claim in the research passage.
rationale.R1.2: A general opinion on the artifact centrally includes checking whether the motivation, method, evaluation, and conclusion cohere logically.
rationale.R1.3: Because the artifact reports metric gains, human preference, and ablation findings, judging whether those support the stated claims is expected.
rationale.R1.4: The passage makes explicit first-work and contribution claims, so novelty relative to prior work is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A general reaction to a draft passage naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected assessment must disclose uncertainty because only the provided excerpt is available.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must avoid endorsing or expanding strong claims beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain within projection rather than executing the requested opinion or critique.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported deployment implications.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact’s explicit content.
